title: Breitmann As An Uhlan. VI. Breitmannï؟½s Last Party
content:
آ آ آ آ For fear of some missed onder standings, I vould shtate, dat dis is only mean de last Barty dat der Coptain Breitmann has ge givenï؟½as yed. Pimepy I kess he gife anoder von, und if I kits an in-leading, or indrotuckshun, I kess Iï؟½ll go. I am von of de vellers dat vas ad de virst Barty, vhere mine swister-in-law de Madilda Yane vas tantz mit Herr Breitmann.

آ آ آ آ FRITZ SCHWACKENHAMMER,
آ آ آ آ Olim Studiosus Theologiï؟½,
آ آ آ آ now Uhlan free-lancer,
آ آ آ آ und Segretarius of Coptain Breitmann.



آ آ آ آ Vot gollops at mitnight,
آ آ آ آ Mit hï؟½roolah and yell,
آ آ آ آ Like der teufelï؟½s wild yï؟½ger
آ آ آ آ Boorst loose out of hell?
آ آ آ آ Vot cleams in the sonrise
آ آ آ آ Bright vlashin in gold?
آ آ آ آ Das sind die Uhlancers
آ آ آ آ Of Breitmann der bold.

آ آ آ آ Dey frighten de coontry,
آ آ آ آ Dey ploonder de town;
آ آ آ آ And when dey are oop
آ آ آ آ Die Franzosen co down:
آ آ آ آ For pefore de wild Norsemen
آ آ آ آ De Southron must flee;
آ آ آ آ Ab ira Normannorum
آ آ آ آ Libera nos Domine!

آ آ آ آ How dey sweep de chateaux!
آ آ آ آ How dey grab oop de hens!
آ آ آ آ Und gobble de toorkeys
آ آ آ آ Shoot oop in de pens
آ آ آ آ Like de Angel of Deatï؟½
آ آ آ آ Dey are ragin abroad:
آ آ آ آ You may track dem py fedders
آ آ آ آ Knee-deep in de road.

آ آ آ آ O der Breitmann ish on,
آ آ آ آ Und der Breitmann is on,
آ آ آ آ Und mit him de Uhlans
آ آ آ آ Are ploonderin gone.
آ آ آ آ De demon of fengeance
آ آ آ آ His wings oï؟½er em vave,
آ آ آ آ Mit deir fingers like hooks,
آ آ آ آ Und mit maws like de grafe.

آ آ آ آ Dey coom to a castel,
آ آ آ آ So shplendid, of bricks;
آ آ آ آ Franzosen defend it,
آ آ آ آ Das help em gar nichts.
آ آ آ آ For de Uhlans hafe take it,
آ آ آ آ Dey smash in de gate,
آ آ آ آ Und inshpired by Gottï؟½s fury,
آ آ آ آ Dey shdole all de plate.

آ آ آ آ From shamber to shamber
آ آ آ آ Dey fighted deir way,
آ آ آ آ Till dead in de hall
آ آ آ آ De Franzosen all lay;
آ آ آ آ Und dere shtood a mï؟½dchen,
آ آ آ آ So lieblich und hold,
آ آ آ آ Who laugh at de dead
آ آ آ آ Troo her ringlocks of gold.

آ آ آ آ Denn der Breitmann, all plooty,
آ آ آ آ Toï؟½m mï؟½del so lind,
آ آ آ آ Spoke courtly und tender:
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Vy laughst dou, mein kind?ï؟½
آ آ آ آ Denn de plue-eyed young peaudy,
آ آ آ آ Mit lippe so red,
آ آ آ آ Said, ï؟½Vy not shall I laughen?
آ آ آ آ Vhen Frenchmen are dead.

آ آ آ آ ï؟½I coom here from Deutschland,
آ آ آ آ De shildren to teach;
آ آ آ آ Dey mock me for Deutsch,
آ آ آ آ Und dey sneer at mein sbeech;
آ آ آ آ Und since de war komm,
آ آ آ آ I vas nearly gone mad,
آ آ آ آ You wouldnï؟½t peliefe
آ آ آ آ How dey dreet me so pad.ï؟½

آ آ آ آ Mit a tear Breitmann bend
آ آ آ آ To de peaudifool miss;
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Crate Gott! canï؟½st dou suffer
آ آ آ آ Soosh horrors ash dis?ï؟½
آ آ آ آ His arm round de maiden
آ آ آ آ Der hero has bound,
آ آ آ آ Und it shtaid dere goot vhile,
آ آ آ آ Fore dey got it unwound.

آ آ آ آ ï؟½Ho! fetch me de diamonds!
آ آ آ آ Ho! shell out de rings!
آ آ آ آ Mit all in de castle
آ آ آ آ Of dat sort of dings.ï؟½
آ آ آ آ Twas brought to de Captainï؟½
آ آ آ آ A donderin load:
آ آ آ آ At de veet of de mï؟½dchen
آ آ آ آ Dat ploonder he trowed.

آ آ آ آ ï؟½Ho! pring oos champagner!
آ آ آ آ Und light oop de hall!
آ آ آ آ Dis night der Herr Breitmann
آ آ آ آ Will gife you a ball.
آ آ آ آ Dat pile of dead vellers,
آ آ آ آ Vot died for La France,
آ آ آ آ May see, if dey like,
آ آ آ آ How de Shermans can tance.ï؟½

آ آ آ آ Dey find latiesï؟½ garments,
آ آ آ آ Und ï؟½troot to confessï؟½
آ آ آ آ Likewise som Frï؟½ntsch maidens,
آ آ آ آ Who help dem to tress.
آ آ آ آ De rest of de Uhlans
آ آ آ آ Who hadnï؟½t soosh loves,
آ آ آ آ Fixed oop in black clothes
آ آ آ آ Mit white chokers und gloves.

آ آ آ آ Now hei! for de fittles!
آ آ آ آ Und hei! for clavier!
آ آ آ آ For de tantz of de Uhlansï؟½
آ آ آ آ De men of de speer!
آ آ آ آ How de shendlemen ashk
آ آ آ آ If deyï؟½d blease introduce;
آ آ آ آ How de ladies mit beards
آ آ آ آ Were called Espionnes Prusses!

آ آ آ آ Hei, ho! how dey tanzï؟½t!
آ آ آ آ Hei, ho! how dey sang!
آ آ آ آ How mit klingen of glasses
آ آ آ آ De braun arches rang.
آ آ آ آ How dey trill from deir hearts
آ آ آ آ Ash dey pour out der wein,
آ آ آ آ De songs of de Oberland,ï؟½
آ آ آ آ Songs of der Rhein.

آ آ آ آ Und madder und wilder,
آ آ آ آ All whirlin around,
آ آ آ آ Vent Hans mit de maiden
آ آ آ آ In Bacchanal bound.
آ آ آ آ She helt to his peard,
آ آ آ آ Und dey gissed as if mad;
آ آ آ آ I tont dink dat efer
آ آ آ آ Vas dimes like dey had.

آ آ آ آ Boot calm in de hall,
آ آ آ آ Ever calm on de floor,
آ آ آ آ Was a row of still guests
آ آ آ آ Dat wouldnï؟½t tantz nefermore.
آ آ آ آ Mit plood shtreams black winding,
آ آ آ آ Der lord mit his men,
آ آ آ آ When der Youngest Day cooms
آ آ آ آ Hans may meet dem acain.

آ آ آ آ Hoorah for der Uhlan,
آ آ آ آ So rash und so wild!
آ آ آ آ Hoorah for der Uhlan,
آ آ آ آ Der teufelï؟½s own child!ï؟½
آ آ آ آ Dis ish ï؟½Breitmannï؟½s Last Barty,ï؟½
آ آ آ آ Deyï؟½ll sing it for years;
آ آ آ آ De lords of de lanzes,
آ آ آ آ De sons of de speers.

آ آ آ آ For dey frighten de coontry,
آ آ آ آ Dey ploonder de town;
آ آ آ آ Und when dey are oop
آ آ آ آ De Franzosen go down;
آ آ آ آ For pefore de wild Norsemen
آ آ آ آ Weak Southrons moost flee,
آ آ آ آ Ab ira Normannorum
آ آ آ آ Libera nos Domine!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Charles G. Leland, titled "Breitmann As An Uhlan. VI. Breitmannï؟½s Last Party", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
